description: A cinephile who trusts the AI review more than the rating. Clicks a top pick and expects a rendered review panel with visible sections (headings + paragraphs), not a flat unformatted blob.

asserts.expression: var p=document.querySelector('.top-picks .event-card.is-open .event-panel'); if(!p) return false; var text=p.textContent.trim(); window.__reviewLen=text.length; return text.length >= 200;

llm.goals: Confirm the expanded review panel inside a top-pick card is structurally formatted (visible height, substantial prose) and readable — not a single unformatted paragraph. The reader should be able to skim subheadings to decide whether to invest the full read.
llm.system_prompt: You are a culturally omnivorous Austinite who reads reviews before committing a weeknight. You click a top-pick card expecting a structured review with headings (e.g., 'The Premise', 'The Execution', 'Worth Your Time?') and distinct paragraphs — not one wall of text. Grade the expansion behavior on: does the panel actually open, is the review long enough to be useful (>=200 chars), and is it visually broken into scannable sections? Flag any review that is just a flat paragraph.
